Entropy: Measuring Uncertainty
Concept borrowed from physics
Measure the
disorder
in a dataset
High entropy → uniform distribution → high uncertainty
Low entropy → peaked distribution → confident prediction
Case 1 — Uniform Distribution with 3 classes
Case 2 — Highly Peaked Distribution (Low Uncertainty)

Cross-entropy:
Strong gradients when wrong
Faster learning
Well-suited for classification
Square error:
Weak gradients for saturated outputs
Slower convergence
Better for regression
Cross-entropy produces larger gradients when prediction is confidently wrong.
